home.meta_description: Ethlathini Rest Camp — overland campsites in a mahogany and fig forest, 2km from Memorial Gate, Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park. Big 5 country. KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa.
home.hero_sub_line1: From tar road to forest canopy in one turn.
home.hero_sub_line2: Self-contained overland campsites in a mahogany & fig forest,
home.hero_badge: 🌍 Africa's oldest game reserve · Est. 1895 · 2nd oldest in the world
home.split_p1: Ethlathini sits on a hillside off the main road to Memorial Gate — a roadside surprise you'd never expect. Pull in and you're under the canopy of mahogany, wild fig, and tree aloe within seconds of leaving the tar.
home.split_p2: Simple shaded overland sites for fully self-contained rigs. Firepits at each site with free forest firewood, clean water points, and WiFi in some areas. Ablutions are under construction — self-sufficient travellers only at this stage.

features.desc: Sleep under a real forest canopy — shade, birdsong, and total bush quiet on a KZN hillside.

features.title: Big 5 at your door
features.desc: 2km from Memorial Gate. Lion, leopard, elephant, rhino & buffalo in Africa's oldest reserve.

features.title: Overland-ready sites
features.desc: Shaded natural sites for fully self-contained rooftop tents, trailers & large rigs.

features.title: Birding paradise
features.desc: Exceptional birdlife with a huge variety of species right on the property.

features.title: Coffee shop & nursery
features.desc: Opening very soon — good coffee in the forest, and indigenous plants to take home.

features.title: Community at heart
features.desc: Skills centre, food garden, fish farm & restaurant — building it one layer at a time.

features.title: Honest & growing

home.roadmap_p2: We tell you what's open and what isn't. The forest is real, the welcome is warm, and everything else is on its way.
home.cta_h2: Ready to camp in the forest?

about.meta_title: About — Forest Campsite in Hluhluwe, KZN
about.meta_description: Ethlathini Rest Camp is a mahogany and fig forest campsite 2km from Memorial Gate, Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park. Our story, our community, our honest journey.
about.p_intro: Nestled in a lush canopy of mahogany, wild fig, and tree aloe on a hillside just off the main road to Memorial Gate, Ethlathini Rest Camp is a growing eco-friendly destination rooted in KwaZulu-Natal and the heart of the Hluhluwe community.
about.p_place: The property sits on a hillside with the forest canopy breaking through. Tree aloes stand tall above the fig trees, and the mahogany provide deep, cool shade for the campsites below. When we arrived, the land was completely overgrown — chromolaena and lantana had taken over. We are still fighting that bush today, clearing metre by metre.
about.p_now1: Today we have four cleared areas for overland campers — natural bush clearings for fully self-contained travellers with rooftop tents, trailers, and overlanding rigs. Each site has a firepit with free firewood, clean water points, and WiFi in selected areas.
about.p_now2: Ablution facilities are under development — self-sufficient overlanders only at this stage.
about.p_book_link: Ready to see it for yourself? <link>Book an overland site</link> and come park under the canopy.
about.p_location: Just 2km from Memorial Gate, Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park — Africa's oldest proclaimed game reserve (established 1895). Big 5 country, the last major stronghold of the southern white rhino, and one of the most biodiverse wilderness areas on the continent.

facts.label: Coming:
facts.value: Chalets, coffee shop, nursery, restaurant, skills center, fish farm, community garden

about.signoff: Siyakwamukela. Welcome. We are glad you are here.

vision.meta_title: Our Vision — Skills Hub & Eco-Social Enterprise, Hluhluwe
vision.meta_description: Ethlathini is building a living classroom in the forest — hospitality, agriculture and future skills development for the Hluhluwe community. See the vision.
vision.hero_sub: A skills hub in the forest — hospitality, agriculture, technology.
vision.plan_desc: This is the vision — a living classroom nestled in the forest canopy. The aerial plan shows how Ethlathini will grow into a complete eco-social destination with campsites, chalets, skills center, fish farm, coffee shop, nursery, and community garden.
vision.plan_note: 📍 View from above — watch the forest and the dream grow together.
vision.pillars_sub: Ethlathini is being built as a living classroom — a place where local youth and community members gain real, marketable skills.
vision.pillars_book_link: Curious what it's like on the ground? <link>Book a campsite</link> and see the first pillar — hospitality — in action.

pillars.title: Hospitality Skills
pillars.desc: The campsites, coffee shop, restaurant, and future chalets will train people in guest relations, housekeeping, lodge management, and food & beverage service.

pillars.title: Trade & Agricultural Skills
pillars.desc: The community garden, fish farm, nursery, and workshop will train people in sustainable food production, aquaculture, indigenous plant propagation, and carpentry, plumbing, and welding.

pillars.title: Future Skills
pillars.desc: The conference center will train people in IT, AI fundamentals, events management, and digital literacy. It will also host weddings, functions and corporate events.

vision.cta_p: Book a site, volunteer, or just come see what we are building.

dream.meta_description: A second chance, a community that believed, a purpose found. The honest story behind Ethlathini Rest Camp in Hluhluwe, KwaZulu-Natal.
dream.hero_sub: A second chance. A community that believed. A purpose found.
dream.p1: We came to Hluhluwe carrying a dream. But somewhere along the way, we trusted the wrong people. Slowly, they stripped away our direction and our hope. We drifted far from the path we were meant to walk.
dream.p2_post: came into our lives — and with them, the truth about the people we had been trusting. By the time they entered our lives, we had nothing left to offer but a story of failure and hearts that still believed in something better.
dream.p3_pre: Yet they opened their land to us. They asked nothing in return. They simply asked one question:
dream.p3_em: "Can you build some kind of skills development center?"
dream.p4: That question brought us back to life. Because that was the dream we had before we lost our way — a place where people could learn, grow, and find their own path. A place that gave back to the community that was now giving us a second chance.
dream.p5: When we first set foot on this property, it was completely overgrown — bush so thick you could not see the buildings. Some structures were dilapidated, roofs caved in, walls crumbling. Invasive species had taken over, choking out the indigenous growth.
dream.p6: We are still fighting that bush today. But slowly, with limited resources and a lot of sweat, we have been clearing, cleaning, and fixing. Every patch of ground we open up is a small victory.
dream.p7: We have two rooms available in the main house for volunteers who want to be part of this journey — clearing invasive plants, building infrastructure, planting the community garden, and more.
dream.p8_pre: If you want to do real work that matters, in a forest, next to a game reserve, with a community that will welcome you —
dream.p9: The forest is real, the welcome is warm, and everything else is growing step by step with love, limited resources, and a community that refuses to let this dream fail.
dream.p10: We lost everything once. But we found something more valuable — a second chance, a community that believed in us, and a purpose that was always waiting for us to come back to it.
dream.p11_pre: Ready to be part of what happens next?
dream.p11_post: and support the dream in person.
dream.signoff: Siyakwamukela. Welcome. We are glad you are here.

faq.meta_title: FAQ — Camping at Ethlathini Rest Camp
faq.meta_description: Frequently asked questions about camping at Ethlathini Rest Camp in Hluhluwe, KZN. Self-contained requirements, facilities, braai, Big 5, booking, and more.
faq.hero_p_pre: Everything you need to know before you visit. Can't find your answer?

faqs.q: Do I need a fully self-contained rig to camp at Ethlathini?
faqs.a: Yes — for now. Ablution facilities are currently under construction. We can only accommodate overlanders and campers who are fully self-contained with their own toilet and water supply. If you have a rooftop tent, camper van, or caravan with onboard facilities, you are very welcome. We are building ablutions as quickly as we can — follow us on social media for updates.

faqs.q: How far is Ethlathini from Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Memorial Gate?
faqs.a: We are 2km from Memorial Gate on the main road leading directly to the gate. You can be inside Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park — Africa's oldest proclaimed game reserve — within 5 minutes of leaving camp. The park is open from 05:00 to 19:00 (summer) and 06:00 to 18:00 (winter).

faqs.q: Is Ethlathini suitable for large overland rigs and rooftop tents?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Our sites are specifically designed for overlanders. We have cleared, level areas that can accommodate large 4x4 vehicles with rooftop tents, trailer setups, and full overland rigs including extended trailers and awnings. We also have a group site for convoys and 4x4 clubs with space for up to 3 vehicles and 8 people.

faqs.q: What facilities are available at Ethlathini Rest Camp?
faqs.a: Currently open: 4 cleared overland campsites, firepits at each site with free firewood from the forest, clean water points, power points in the main house for charging devices, WiFi in selected areas, and a portable braai. Coming soon: ablutions and showers, more campsites, chalets, a coffee shop and nursery, a restaurant, and a community skills centre. We are honest about what is and is not ready yet.

faqs.q: Can I braai / make a fire at my campsite?
faqs.a: Yes — every site has a designated firepit and we provide free firewood from the forest. We ask that fires are kept within the designated pits only, and that generators and excessive noise stop at 22:00. Quiet hours are 22:00–06:00.

faqs.q: What is there to do near Ethlathini Rest Camp?
faqs.a: The main attraction is Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park — Africa's oldest game reserve (established 1895) and a Big 5 destination just 2km away. The park is world-famous for its white rhino population and wildlife density. Beyond the park: Hluhluwe town (10 minutes) has fuel, shops, restaurants, and ATMs. iSimangaliso Wetland Park (UNESCO World Heritage Site) and St Lucia are about 45 minutes away. The Zululand birding route passes through the area.

faqs.q: How do I book a campsite — is there an online booking system?
faqs.a: Yes — use the Book & Rates page on this website to submit a booking request. We confirm availability and send a deposit invoice within 4 hours (07:00–20:00). You can also WhatsApp us directly at {phone} for a faster response. We require a 50% deposit to confirm your booking, with the balance payable on arrival.

"Ethlathini" is an isiZulu word meaning "in the forest". It perfectly describes the property — a mahogany, wild fig, and tree aloe forest on a hillside just off the main road to Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park. The name was chosen to honour the land and the isiZulu heritage of the Hluhluwe community.
Yes — well-behaved dogs are welcome on a lead. Please be mindful of other campers and the wildlife in the area. Note that pets are not permitted inside Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park itself.

faqs.q: Is there power at the campsites?
faqs.a: Currently there are power points available in the main house for charging phones, laptops, and batteries. There is no mains power at the individual campsites yet. We recommend bringing a portable power station or solar setup for extended stays. Power to individual sites is on our development roadmap.

faqs.q: How close are supplies and fuel?
faqs.a: Hluhluwe town is approximately 10 minutes away and has a Shoprite supermarket, Engen and BP fuel stations, an ATM, pharmacy, KFC, Debonairs, and various local restaurants. We strongly recommend filling up with fuel and stocking up on supplies before arriving, especially if you plan to spend multiple days game-viewing.

Yes — we welcome volunteers who want to be part of building something meaningful in the Hluhluwe community. We have volunteer rooms available in the main house. Tasks range from clearing invasive plants and building infrastructure to gardening and community skills development. Visit our Volunteer page for full details.

faq.sidebar_vol_p: Be part of building something meaningful in Hluhluwe.

volunteer.meta_title: Volunteer — Be Part of the Build, Hluhluwe KZN
volunteer.meta_description: Volunteer at Ethlathini Rest Camp in Hluhluwe, KwaZulu-Natal. Eco camp volunteer program, workaway South Africa, skills development, community garden, Big 5 game reserve.
volunteer.eyebrow: Voluntourism · Workaway South Africa · Eco Camp
volunteer.hero_p: Help us clear, build, plant, and grow an eco-social destination in the heart of KwaZulu-Natal — 2km from Africa's oldest game reserve. No experience required. Just willingness.
volunteer.honestBanner: Honest about what this is: We're not a polished volunteer resort. We're a property being built from scratch by a small team with limited resources and a big vision. If you want comfortable tourism with light volunteering — this is not it. If you want to do real work that matters, in a forest, next to a game reserve, with a community that will welcome you — come.

tasks.title: Clearing invasive plants
tasks.desc: Chromolaena, lantana, and prickly pear are being cleared metre by metre to restore indigenous forest. Physical, rewarding, and directly visible progress.

tasks.title: Building infrastructure
tasks.desc: Ablutions, site improvements, fencing, signage, and general property maintenance. Basic tools provided — building experience a bonus but not required.

tasks.title: Community food garden
tasks.desc: Planting, watering, weeding, and maintaining the food garden that feeds the community development programme.

tasks.title: Fish farm development
tasks.desc: Help set up aquaculture infrastructure as part of the community skills programme.

tasks.title: Skills development support
tasks.desc: If you have skills in hospitality, IT, agriculture, trades, or teaching — there is meaningful work here training the next generation.

tasks.title: Forest restoration
tasks.desc: Propagating indigenous trees and plants in the nursery, replanting cleared areas, and general forest care.

tasks.title: Wildlife monitoring & rehabilitation
tasks.desc: Assist with wildlife tracking, data collection, and rehabilitation efforts in and around Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park. Learn about conservation first-hand.

tasks.title: Community schools & educational activities
tasks.desc: Support local pre-schools and community schools with teaching, reading programmes, arts and crafts, and educational activities that make a real difference.

volunteer.benefits_sub: We offer paid volunteer packages that include accommodation, meals, transport, and activities — fully customised to your interests and availability. This is a true partnership — your contribution directly strengthens Ethlathini, uplifts the Bhejane community, and supports conservation across the greater Big Five Hlabisa region, all while immersing you in the real KwaZulu-Natal.

package_includes.label: Accommodation in the main house (shared bathroom)
Transport to and from local activities
Access to Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park (2km away) on your days off
Live and work in a mahogany and fig forest
Reference letter on completion of a meaningful stay
Coffee shop access when it opens

volunteer.stayCard_p: We offer customised volunteer packages to suit your needs. Accommodation, meals, transport, and activities are all included.
custom_activities: Cultural experiences with the Bhejane community
volunteer.story_p1_pre: Ethlathini was born from a second chance. The property was overgrown, the buildings dilapidated, and the land reclaimed by invasive species. The Mdledshe family opened the land with one question:
volunteer.story_p1_em: "Can you build a skills development centre?"
volunteer.story_p2: That question is still driving everything. The campsites fund the build. The build creates the skills centre. The skills centre trains the community. Volunteers are part of that chain — not tourists observing it.
volunteer.story_p3: Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Park — Africa's oldest game reserve — is 2km down the road. The Bhejane and Mdledshe communities surround us. This is a real place, with real stakes, and room for real people who want to contribute.
volunteer.cta_p: WhatsApp or email us with a short introduction — who you are, when you're available, what you'd like to contribute, and what kind of package you're interested in. No CV needed. Just be honest.
volunteer.cta_note: We reply to all enquiries within 48 hours · Custom packages available · Spaces are limited
